paper_analysis.objective: To determine steady states of a semi-infinite solid subject to the Grinfeld instability in systems with a fixed wavelength, using amplitude expansions.
paper_analysis.method: The paper employs high-order weakly nonlinear amplitude expansions, using system size as a control parameter to circumvent the absence of an instability threshold in extended systems without gravity. The dependence of solution branch structures on gravity levels is analyzed.
key_findings: A transition from supercritical to subcritical bifurcation occurs as gravity increases or nonhydrostatic stress decreases.
A discrete, possibly infinite, set of solution branches exists at given system parameters.
The approach is restricted to relatively small nondimensional amplitudes (≲ 0.2).
limitations: The method is limited to small nondimensional amplitudes (≲ 0.2).
The approach does not address larger amplitudes, which will be tackled in a companion paper.
paper_analysis.context: This work contributes to the understanding of nonlinear dynamics in semi-infinite solids, particularly in the context of Grinfeld instability and its dependence on gravity.
known_analogues: reminiscent of dendritic or eutectic growth
